dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I've done something that haunts me. I need help understanding what happened before I lose my mind.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Three nights ago, the guard patrol I was leading encountered a group of cloaked figures {location_hint}. They moved like {enemy_type} scouts: same formations, same signals. We engaged. It was over in seconds. When we pulled back the hoods, we found the faces of traders from a neighboring settlement. Friendly traders. Disguised, apparently, to avoid bandit attention on the roads. {target_npc} identified them from their goods: they were bringing {quest_item} to {location} as a gift. I killed allies carrying gifts.
dialogueChunks.outro: I need someone to travel to that settlement and find out the full truth. Were they really traders? Why the disguises? And how do I face their families?

playerResponses.text: You acted on what you saw. Let me find out the full story before you condemn yourself.
Killing disguised allies is a nightmare. I'll get answers, but you have to face whatever I find.
That's a burden you'll have to carry alone. I can't undo what happened.

dialogueChunks.intro: I haven't slept in four days, {player_name}. Every time I close my eyes I see their faces.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: There was a creature terrorizing the roads near {location}: huge, aggressive, wearing scraps of {enemy_type} armor. I tracked it for two days {location_hint} and finally brought it down in a ravine. When I examined the body, I found a necklace that {target_npc} recognized. It belonged to a villager who went missing months ago. The alchemist says they were transformed: some kind of {quest_item} compound that warped their body beyond recognition. I killed someone I should have been trying to save.
dialogueChunks.outro: Someone turned a villager into a monster. I need to know who, and I need to find out if there are others before I unknowingly kill them too.

playerResponses.text: If someone is transforming people into creatures, we need to stop them. I'll investigate.
You couldn't have known. But there might be more victims out there. I'll look.
Transformation curses are beyond my expertise. Find a healer.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, I came to {location} looking for a friend. Instead I found out what I've done.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Last month I cleared a {enemy_type} camp {location_hint} on a bounty. Standard work: burned the supplies, scattered the survivors. What I didn't know was that the camp held prisoners: people from nearby settlements being used as forced labor. {target_npc} told me one of the prisoners was a herbalist who had been gathering {quest_item} under duress. When I torched the supply tent, the herbalist was still inside. I didn't check. I need to go back and search the ruins for survivors, or at least for proof of what really happened there.
dialogueChunks.outro: I was so focused on completing the bounty that I never thought to look for captives. That has to change, starting now.

playerResponses.text: Captives held in an enemy camp: that changes everything. I'll help you search the ruins.
You rushed a job and people suffered. I'll help find the truth, but you need to face it.
What's done is done. Going back won't bring anyone back.

dialogueChunks.intro: Don't touch that pile, {player_name}. Those are the belongings of someone I wronged. I can't bring myself to move them.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: A week ago, I intercepted a smuggler's cart {location_hint} carrying what I thought was stolen {quest_item}. The driver fought back and I... I didn't hold back. Turns out the cart belonged to a healer from a neighboring settlement. The {quest_item} was medicine, not contraband, bound for people dying of plague. {target_npc} confirmed the healer had legitimate passage papers that I never asked to see. Now an entire village is waiting for medicine that will never arrive, and the person carrying it is dead because of me.
dialogueChunks.outro: I need to deliver what's left of that medicine and find out if it's too late for the people who needed it. I owe them that much.

playerResponses.text: The medicine still needs to reach those people. I'll make the delivery.
You killed a healer carrying medicine. That's heavy. But the sick still need help.
You can't undo a killing with a delivery. Carry that weight yourself.
